title: Packet Detective
learning_objectives: Identify network protocols and ports from packet captures
Analyse TCP handshakes and connection states
Detect network anomalies from traffic patterns
scenario: Your team lead hands you a PCAP file from a suspicious network session captured at a client site. Before using any automated tools, she wants proof you understand what you are looking at. Identify protocols, map communications, and spot anomalies.

scenario_context.challenge: Manual PCAP analysis demonstrating protocol understanding
scenario_context.relevance: Network traffic analysis is essential for both offensive and defensive operations. Understanding what normal traffic looks like helps identify malicious activity.

subtasks.title: Protocol Identification
subtasks.description: Identify all protocols present and list the ports they use. Note any non-standard port usage.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Open the PCAP. List every protocol observed. For each, note the port number and whether it's the standard port. Flag any protocol running on a non-standard port — this is often suspicious.
subtasks.evidence_required: Protocol identification table: port, protocol, standard/non-standard

subtasks.title: Handshake Analysis
subtasks.description: Find all TCP handshake sequences. Document completed vs abnormal handshakes.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Filter for SYN packets. Trace each connection's handshake: SYN → SYN-ACK → ACK. Note incomplete handshakes, RST packets, and retransmissions. These patterns reveal scanning, firewalls, or issues.
subtasks.evidence_required: Handshake analysis with connection status per pair

subtasks.title: Communication Map
subtasks.description: Draw a communication map showing all IP addresses and connections. Note direction and volume.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create a diagram: each IP as a node, each connection as an arrow with protocol and data volume. Identify the most talkative hosts, any broadcast traffic, and unexpected connections.

subtasks.title: Anomaly Report
subtasks.description: Identify at least 2 potential anomalies and explain why they are suspicious.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Look for: unusual ports, high-frequency connections, DNS to unexpected servers, large data transfers to external IPs, unencrypted credentials, scanning patterns. Explain why each is suspicious.
subtasks.evidence_required: Anomaly report with evidence and reasoning

reflection_prompt: You find evidence of data exfiltration in the PCAP — sensitive data being sent to an external IP over DNS queries. How would you report this to the client, and what immediate actions would you recommend?

quiz.question: A TCP SYN packet without a responding SYN-ACK likely indicates:
options: The port is filtered or the host is down
quiz.explanation: A SYN without SYN-ACK means the connection attempt failed — the port may be filtered by a firewall or the host isn't responding.

quiz.question: A protocol running on a non-standard port is suspicious because:
options: Attackers often use non-standard ports to evade detection
quiz.explanation: Attackers frequently run services on non-standard ports to bypass firewall rules and avoid detection by simple port-based filtering.

mentor_notes: Evaluate understanding over tool proficiency. A student who correctly identifies anomalies with clear reasoning scores higher than one who uses Wireshark features but misses the significance.
